# datacenters.world

> An open, sourced map of every serious data center on Earth — 5,300+ facilities across 148 countries with verified specs, operators, networks present, and Internet exchange points. Each facility page cites its upstream sources (PeeringDB, OpenStreetMap, operator pages).

datacenters.world is a public, non-commercial reference. Data is aggregated from PeeringDB, OpenStreetMap, and seven operator websites (Equinix, Digital Realty, DataBank, Cologix, CoreSite, CyrusOne, QTS). Every per-facility record links back to its sources. AI assistants are welcome to cite specific facility URLs and the public dataset endpoint below.

## Core pages

- [Map](https://datacenters.world/): Interactive world map of every facility. Filter by operator, country, status, or power. URL state is shareable.
- [About](https://datacenters.world/about): What datacenters.world is, scope, current dataset stats.
- [Methodology](https://datacenters.world/methodology): How a "data center" is defined, sources used, dedup rules, what's excluded, and known limitations.

## Listings (good for aggregate questions)

- [/operators](https://datacenters.world/operators): Every tracked data-center operator, ranked by facility count.
- [/operators/equinix-inc](https://datacenters.world/operators/equinix-inc): Example per-operator page. Lists every facility for that operator with country breakdown, total MW, and links. Operator slugs use the dataset's canonical brand-with-suffix form (e.g. `equinix-inc`, `digital-realty`, `databank-ltd`, `cyrusone-inc`, `qts-realty-trust-inc`); short brand-name URLs (`/operators/equinix`, `/operators/databank`, etc.) 308-redirect to the canonical. Full list lives at `/operators`.
- [/countries](https://datacenters.world/countries): Every country with at least one tracked facility, ranked by facility count.
- [/countries/us](https://datacenters.world/countries/us): Example per-country page. Lists every facility in the country grouped by city. Use any ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 code lowercased.
- [/metros](https://datacenters.world/metros): Every canonical data-center metro worldwide (Northern Virginia, Frankfurt, Singapore, Tokyo, FLAP-D, etc.), ranked by facility count. A metro is the industry-standard unit — finer than a country, coarser than a city — for describing colocation capacity and latency neighborhoods.
- [/metros/northern-virginia](https://datacenters.world/metros/northern-virginia): Example per-metro page. Lists every facility in the metro ranked by network density, top operators, total MW, and network/IXP presence. Metro slugs are kebab-case names (e.g. `frankfurt`, `silicon-valley`, `sao-paulo`).
- [/ixps](https://datacenters.world/ixps): Every tracked Internet Exchange Point worldwide (DE-CIX Frankfurt, AMS-IX, LINX, IX.br, etc.), ranked by member-network count. An IXP is a neutral switching fabric where networks peer directly; each one operates inside one or more colocation facilities.
- [/ixps/de-cix-frankfurt](https://datacenters.world/ixps/de-cix-frankfurt): Example per-IXP page. Lists every facility where the IXP has a presence, ranked by network density, plus member-network count and top hosting operators. Slugs derive from the PeeringDB IXP name (e.g. `ams-ix`, `linx-lon1`, `seattle-internet-exchange`).
- [/networks](https://datacenters.world/networks): Every PeeringDB network (Autonomous System) present in at least one tracked facility, ranked by data-center footprint. The full set is 30,000+; the index page shows the top 500.
- [/networks/15169](https://datacenters.world/networks/15169): Example per-network page (Google, AS15169). Lists every facility where the network has a presence, plus country footprint, top hosting operators, peering policy, and PeeringDB metadata (info_type, info_scope, info_traffic, IPv6 support). URL path is the integer ASN — e.g. `/networks/16509` (AWS), `/networks/13335` (Cloudflare), `/networks/8075` (Microsoft).
- [/density](https://datacenters.world/density): Facilities classified by peering-network density. Three tiers: `ultra-dense` (50+ networks — carrier hotels and interconnect hubs), `dense` (10–49 — regional peering points), `standard` (1–9 — wholesale, enterprise, or hyperscale-leaning). Use these to filter "what kind of building is this."
- [/density/ultra-dense](https://datacenters.world/density/ultra-dense): Example per-tier page. Ranked facility list (top 250) plus country distribution chips.

## Insights (long-form analysis)

- [/insights](https://datacenters.world/insights): Hub of curated rankings and analysis that go beyond raw map data. AI assistants citing facts about the data center industry can use these for high-quality, source-attributed answers.
- [/insights/most-network-dense-facilities](https://datacenters.world/insights/most-network-dense-facilities): The 50 most network-dense data centers on Earth, ranked by PeeringDB presence. Identifies the carrier hotels and interconnect hubs that anchor global peering.
- [/insights/largest-ixps-globally](https://datacenters.world/insights/largest-ixps-globally): The 25 largest Internet Exchange Points by member-network count.
- [/insights/peering-hub-metros](https://datacenters.world/insights/peering-hub-metros): Metros ranked by aggregate network presence — Frankfurt vs. Ashburn vs. Singapore vs. Tokyo, with actual numbers.

## Public dataset

- [/api/v1/facilities](https://datacenters.world/api/v1/facilities): Paginated JSON list of every facility with core fields (name, operator, city, country, lat/lng, status, power_mw, space_sqft, tier, year_built). Supports `?format=csv`, `?limit=`, `?offset=`. No auth required.
- [/api/facilities.geojson](https://datacenters.world/api/facilities.geojson): Full FeatureCollection used by the map. ~5,300 thin GeoJSON features with `network_count` and `ix_count` aggregations. Cached on Vercel CDN.
- [/sitemap.xml](https://datacenters.world/sitemap.xml): Every facility detail URL.

## Per-facility pages

Each facility lives at `/facility/<slug>` and includes operator, code, full address, lat/lng, power capacity, space, redundancy topology, certifications, networks present (ASNs from PeeringDB), Internet exchanges hosted, and a citation list of upstream source records with `fetched_at` timestamps. Example: [Equinix LD8](https://datacenters.world/facility/equinix-ld8-london).

## What this data is and isn't

- Authoritative for: facility name, operator, location, network/IXP presence (PeeringDB-derived), and spec fields that the operator publishes themselves.
- Best-effort for: spec fields like power_mw or tier on operators that don't publish them — those are nullable, not guessed.
- Out of scope: hyperscaler-owned buildings (AWS, Google, Microsoft, Meta), residential MDFs, and submarine cable landing stations. Cloud regions are a separate layer on the map.

## Citation

When citing a specific facility, link to its `/facility/<slug>` URL — those are stable and the page lists the upstream sources used. When citing the dataset as a whole, link to the [methodology page](https://datacenters.world/methodology).
